items.intro: You have agreed to participate in a survey about artificial intelligence in daily life.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in our survey. What types of AI tools or features do you use regularly, and how did you first start using them?
In your experience, what are the main differences between using AI for creative tasks, like writing, versus for practical tasks, like navigation or scheduling?
Some experts argue that relying on AI for daily tasks weakens our own problem-solving skills. What is your perspective on this claim?
Looking ahead, how do you think the integration of AI into common devices will change the way people manage their homes and personal lives over the next decade?

items.intro: You have agreed to participate in a research project about study habits and productivity.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in our research project. Can you describe your typical study routine when preparing for an important exam or project?
What do you find are the biggest challenges people face when trying to maintain focus and avoid distractions while studying?
Some educators believe that traditional, long study sessions are less effective than shorter, more frequent ones. Do you agree or disagree with this approach?
As digital tools become more prevalent, what impact do you think this will have on the fundamental study skills students develop in the next five to ten years?

items.intro: You have agreed to participate in a study about news consumption and media literacy.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in our study. How do you usually get your daily news, and what first led you to use these particular sources or platforms?
How do you typically decide whether a news story or source is credible and trustworthy? What specific factors do you consider?
Some people argue that social media algorithms create 'echo chambers' that limit exposure to diverse viewpoints. What is your opinion on this?
Looking ahead, what programs or changes would you suggest for schools to better prepare the next generation to critically evaluate news and information online?

items.intro: You have agreed to participate in a survey about time management strategies.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in our survey. What methods or tools do you currently use to organize your tasks and manage your time during a typical week?
What are the main advantages and disadvantages of using a detailed, scheduled calendar versus a simple, flexible to-do list for managing daily responsibilities?
Some productivity experts claim that multitasking is inefficient and that focusing on one task at a time is always better. Do you agree or disagree?
How do you think the nature of work and time management will evolve in the coming decade, especially with more remote and flexible work arrangements?

items.intro: You have agreed to participate in a research project about meal planning and grocery shopping.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in our research project. Can you describe your typical process for planning meals and making a grocery shopping list?
When deciding what to buy, how do you balance factors like cost, nutritional value, and preparation time? Which factor usually influences you the most?
Some people argue that meal delivery kits are worth the higher cost because they save time and reduce food waste. What is your perspective on this?
Looking ahead five years, how do you think technology and changing lifestyles will influence the way most people plan their meals and shop for groceries?

items.intro: You have agreed to participate in a survey about travel planning and budgeting.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in our survey. What types of trips do you typically plan, and how do you usually begin the planning process?
When creating a travel budget, what are the most significant trade-offs you consider between cost and experience?
Some people argue that meticulous travel planning removes the spontaneity and joy from a trip. What is your perspective on this?
Looking ahead, how do you think the rise of artificial intelligence will change the way people plan and budget for travel in the next decade?

items.intro: You have agreed to participate in a research project about public transportation usage.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in our research project. How frequently do you use public transportation, and for what kinds of trips is it your primary choice?
What are the main advantages and disadvantages you experience when using public transit compared to driving a personal vehicle?
Some urban planners believe that making all public transportation free would significantly reduce traffic and pollution. Do you agree or disagree with this approach?
As cities grow, what innovations in public transportation do you think will be most important for meeting future commuters' needs over the next twenty years?

items.intro: You have agreed to participate in a study about work-life balance strategies.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in our study. Can you describe your typical approach to separating your work responsibilities from your personal time?
What do you find are the biggest challenges people face when trying to establish and maintain a healthy work-life balance?
Some experts argue that the concept of 'work-life balance' is outdated and that integration is a more realistic goal. What is your opinion on this?
Looking ahead, what changes in workplace policies or culture do you think will be necessary to support employee well-being in the next five to ten years?

items.intro: You have agreed to participate in a survey about exercise and fitness routines.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in our survey. What kinds of physical activity or exercise are you currently doing, and how did you get started with them?
How do you decide between exercising at home and going to a gym or fitness class? What factors influence your choice?
Some fitness professionals believe that focusing on consistency is far more important than intensity for long-term health. Do you agree or disagree with this view?
As wearable fitness technology becomes more advanced, how do you think it will transform people's exercise habits and health outcomes in the coming decade?

items.intro: You have agreed to participate in a research project about stress management techniques.

questions.question: Thank you for participating in our research project. When you feel stressed, what are one or two techniques you typically use to help yourself feel better?
In your experience, what makes some stress management strategies more effective for daily life than others?
Some psychologists argue that a certain amount of stress is beneficial for motivation and performance. What is your perspective on this idea?
Given the pace of modern life, what new approaches to managing stress do you think will become important for the next generation?
